message:
feat(raptorq): replace external raptorq crate with in-house RFC 6330 codec

Remove the external `raptorq` v2.0 dependency and replace it with a
from-scratch, internal RFC 6330 RaptorQ implementation under
`crates/fcp-raptorq/src/codec/`. This gives full control over the
fountain-code layer — a critical data path for erasure-coded storage
and mesh transport.

New internal modules:
- codec/gf256.rs: GF(256) finite field arithmetic with const-evaluated
  log/exp lookup tables
- codec/linalg.rs: dense/sparse row operations for linear algebra over
  GF(256)
- codec/rfc6330.rs: RFC 6330 lookup tables (V0/V1), pseudo-random
  functions, systematic index table
- codec/systematic.rs: systematic encoder with precode matrix, LDPC+HDPC
  constraints, and LT repair symbol generation
- codec/decoder.rs: inactivation decoder (peeling + Gaussian elimination)
- oti.rs: ObjectTransmissionInformation struct (replaces the re-exported
  type from the external crate)

Key behavioral changes:
- Encoder: explicit source symbol splitting with zero-padding, repair
  symbol generation via LT encoding, inner_k_prime() accessor
- Decoder: rewritten to use internal InactivationDecoder with
  HashMap<u32, Vec<u8>> instead of raptorq::Decoder; adds
  try_reconstruct() implementing RFC 6330 symbol assembly with
  constraint symbols and padding
- ESI numbering: repair ESIs now start at K' (per RFC 6330) instead of K
- Golden tests updated to use internal decoder API
  (decoder.add_symbol(esi, data)) instead of raptorq::EncodingPacket
- All downstream crates (fcp-mesh, fcp-store) migrated from
  `raptorq::ObjectTransmissionInformation` to
  `fcp_raptorq::ObjectTransmissionInformation`

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 ]

-
 # WASI runtime

@@ -14,7 +14,7 @@ workspace = true

 blake3.workspace = true
-raptorq.workspace = true

@@ -18,7 +18,6 @@ tracing.workspace = true

@@ -119,7 +119,7 @@ fn encode_payload(
     config: &RaptorQConfig,
 ) -> (
     Vec<(u32, Vec<u8>)>,
-    raptorq::ObjectTransmissionInformation,
+    fcp_raptorq::ObjectTransmissionInformation,
     u32,
 ) {
     let encoder = RaptorQEncoder::new(payload, config).expect("encode");
@@ -133,7 +133,7 @@ fn encode_payload(
